title: A coloured radiative cooling coating with high solar reflectance - Nature Energy
gist: A one-step fabrication process using ethyl cellulose — a biomass-derived material — produces a bilayer coating that delivers vibrant structural colors while maintaining negligible solar absorption and high long-wave infrared emission, enabling sub-ambient daytime cooling of up to 9 degrees Celsius without white-only surfaces.

insights.claim: A one-step-processed bilayer ethyl cellulose coating achieves vibrant structural colors with negligible solar absorption and high long-wave infrared emission, overcoming the longstanding trade-off between aesthetically acceptable coloration and high solar reflectance in radiative cooling materials.
insights.speaker: Nature Energy
insights.so_what: Solving the color-versus-performance trade-off unlocks radiative cooling coatings for the broad commercial building and consumer product markets where all-white surfaces are aesthetically or architecturally unacceptable.
insights.quote: Overcoming the trade-off between colouration and high solar reflectance is a challenge for radiative cooling materials.

insights.claim: The ethyl cellulose bilayer radiative cooling coating enables sub-ambient daytime cooling of up to 9 degrees Celsius under direct sunlight, demonstrating meaningful cooling performance relevant to building energy consumption reduction.
insights.speaker: Nature Energy
insights.so_what: A 9 degree Celsius passive cooling delta is commercially significant for reducing building air conditioning loads, particularly in high-heat urban environments where electricity demand for cooling is a grid stress point.
insights.quote: This coating enables sub-ambient daytime cooling of up to 9 °C under sunlight.

insights.claim: The radiative cooling coating is fabricated from ethyl cellulose, a biomass-derived material, fabricated in a single manufacturing step — reducing both raw material and process costs compared to multi-step coatings using rare-earth phosphors or vacuum-deposited layers.
insights.speaker: Nature Energy
insights.so_what: Biomass-derived single-step fabrication lowers the barrier for commercial scale-up versus competing colored radiative cooling approaches that rely on rare-earth dopants or complex multilayer deposition.
